extraction.one_line_summary: CLARKE Simon won from the breakaway on cobbles Stage 5 after refusing to work with former teammate POWLESS, who nearly took yellow but missed by seconds
tactical_patterns: CLARKE refused cooperation with former teammate POWLESS in finale, conserved energy while others chased, then attacked exhausted group.
CORT Magnus went in break despite zero KOM points available, suggesting team directive to burn rival domestiques.
VAN AERT Wout stayed near GC group instead of chasing break, prioritizing yellow defense over stage hunting.
POWLESS came within 13 seconds of yellow via breakaway on cobbles, revealing EF's GC ambitions beyond mountain stages.

rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: positive: Tactically patient in break, refused to work with stronger Powless, timed attack when group exhausted
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: Nearly took yellow from cobbles breakaway at 25, showing EF has genuine GC rider not just mountain domestique
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Prioritized yellow jersey defense over stage win despite cobbles specialty, uncharacteristic restraint
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: negative: In break every stage including no-KOM-points day, possibly being used to exhaust rather than win
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Stayed within 20 seconds despite dangerous cobbles, better damage limitation than positioning suggested
